# harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule (Resource)

Resource for creating and managing Harness Artifact Registry Lifecycle Rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Account-scoped DELETE rule — keep last 10 versions, runs nightly
resource "harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule" "nightly_cleanup" {
  account_id  = "your-account-id"
  name        = "nightly-cleanup"
  action      = "DELETE"
  description = "Keep last 10 versions of all artifacts"

  apply_to {
    mode = "ALL_IN_SCOPE"
  }

  criteria {
    match = "ALL"
    rules {
      type  = "KEEP_LAST_N"
      value = 10
    }
  }

  schedule {
    expression = "0 2 * * *"
    timezone   = "UTC"
  }
}

# Project-scoped DELETE rule — delete artifacts older than 30 days
resource "harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule" "age_based_cleanup" {
  account_id = "your-account-id"
  org_id     = "your-org-id"
  project_id = "your-project-id"
  name       = "age-based-cleanup"
  action     = "DELETE"

  apply_to {
    mode = "ALL_IN_SCOPE"
  }

  criteria {
    match = "ALL"
    rules {
      type  = "AGE_BASED"
      value = 30
      unit  = "DAYS"
    }
  }
}

# Org-scoped PROTECT rule — protect images in specific registries matching a tag pattern
resource "harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule" "protect_prod" {
  account_id   = "your-account-id"
  org_id       = "your-org-id"
  name         = "protect-prod-images"
  action       = "PROTECT"
  package_type = "DOCKER"

  apply_to {
    mode       = "EXPLICIT"
    registries = ["prod-registry", "release-registry"]
  }

  filter_config {
    package_type             = "DOCKER"
    tag_name_allowed_pattern = ["v*", "release-*"]
  }
}

# Account-scoped DELETE rule with multiple criteria (ANY match)
resource "harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule" "multi_criteria_cleanup" {
  account_id = "your-account-id"
  name       = "multi-criteria-cleanup"
  action     = "DELETE"

  apply_to {
    mode = "ALL_IN_SCOPE"
  }

  criteria {
    match = "ANY"
    rules {
      type  = "KEEP_LAST_N"
      value = 5
    }
    rules {
      type  = "AGE_BASED"
      value = 90
      unit  = "DAYS"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `account_id` (String) Account identifier for the lifecycle rule.
- `action` (String) Action to perform: DELETE or PROTECT.
- `apply_to` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Defines which registries this rule applies to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--apply_to))
- `name` (String) Name of the lifecycle rule.

### Optional

- `criteria` (Block List, Max: 1) Cleanup criteria for the lifecycle rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria))
- `description` (String)
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `filter_config` (Block List, Max: 1) Package-type-specific filter configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--filter_config))
- `org_id` (String) Organization identifier. Required for org-scoped rules.
- `package_type` (String) Package type the rule applies to (e.g. DOCKER, MAVEN, HELM).
- `project_id` (String) Project identifier. Required for project-scoped rules.
- `schedule` (Block List, Max: 1) Cron schedule for automatic execution of the rule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--schedule))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Timestamp when the rule was created (milliseconds since epoch).
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_run_at` (Number) Timestamp of the last execution (milliseconds since epoch).
- `next_run_at` (Number) Timestamp of the next scheduled execution (milliseconds since epoch).
- `rule_id` (String) Unique ID of the lifecycle rule (returned by the API).
- `updated_at` (Number) Timestamp when the rule was last updated (milliseconds since epoch).

<a id="nestedblock--apply_to"></a>
### Nested Schema for `apply_to`

Required:

- `mode` (String) Mode: ALL_IN_SCOPE or EXPLICIT.

Optional:

- `registries` (List of String) List of registry identifiers (required when mode=EXPLICIT).


<a id="nestedblock--criteria"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria`

Required:

- `match` (String) How criteria rules are combined: ALL or ANY.
- `rules` (Block List, Min: 1) List of individual criteria rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--rules))

<a id="nestedblock--criteria--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.rules`

Required:

- `type` (String) Criteria type: KEEP_LAST_N, AGE_BASED, or UNUSED_FOR.

Optional:

- `unit` (String) Time unit for age/unused-for criteria: DAYS, MONTHS, or YEARS.
- `value` (Number) Numeric value for the criteria (e.g. number of versions, number of days).



<a id="nestedblock--filter_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter_config`

Required:

- `package_type` (String) Package type this filter applies to (e.g. DOCKER, MAVEN, HUGGINGFACE).

Optional:

- `dataset_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for HuggingFace dataset names to include (HUGGINGFACE only).
- `group_id_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for Maven group IDs to include (MAVEN only).
- `model_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for HuggingFace model names to include (HUGGINGFACE only).
- `package_name_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for package/image names to include.
- `tag_name_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for Docker tag names to include (DOCKER only).
- `version_name_allowed_pattern` (List of String) Glob patterns for version/tag names to include.


<a id="nestedblock--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Required:

- `expression` (String) Cron expression (e.g. '0 2 * * *').
- `timezone` (String) Timezone for the cron schedule (e.g. 'UTC', 'America/New_York').

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Account level: <account_id>/<rule_id>
terraform import harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule.example <account_id>/<rule_id>

# Org level: <account_id>/<org_id>/<rule_id>
terraform import harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule.example <account_id>/<org_id>/<rule_id>

# Project level: <account_id>/<org_id>/<project_id>/<rule_id>
terraform import harness_platform_har_lifecycle_rule.example <account_id>/<org_id>/<project_id>/<rule_id>
```
